sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The center bar has changed - the gray center of the bar is now gone, and there are now two blue squares separated by a gray area. Let me check the current state.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python

<function=python>
<parameter=code>

for n in after:
    print(f"id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, boundary={n['boundary']}, children={n['children']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I see the center bar has been split into multiple pieces:
- id=4: blue left part [28-31, 28-31]
- id=5: dark gray [28-31, 32-35] with blue center [29-30, 33-34]
- id=6: blue right part [28-31, 36-39]
The center bar has been partially consumed. The middle section (id=5) is now dark gray with a blue center, similar to the original targets. The player is at [33-35, 32-35].
So it seems like the center bar was a compound target that split when I interacted with it. Now I need to collect the remaining pieces. Let me try SPACE again since I'm adjacent to id=5 which is a dark gray box with blue center. Let me try SPACE.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Player at [33-35, 32-35]
# id=5 (dark gray with blue center) at [28-31, 32-35]
# Try SPACE to collect it
action(['SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
